rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2020 per stipulation of both counsel (via e-mail 06/22/2020). *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DILLON T. WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/20. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.6 A.] No proof of service has been filed. Notwithstanding the petition caption, there is no account attached to or filed separately from the petition and no bank statements have been filed. An account for a decedent's estate must always begin with the date of death. The will provides for a testamentary trust to be created; the petition does not address why the estate should be distributed outright to Benita Wong. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if a beneficiary was previously incarcerated. The legal description of the real property must be set forth. [CRC 7.651(b). The request for extraordinary fees does not disclose how much time was spent on ordinary services. [CRC 7.703(a).] After all procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will be required for the Court to determine if $59,950 for preparing and filing delinquent tax returns is reasonable in light of the $34,259.31 statutory fee for an otherwise simple estate in which not assets were sold and no account was filed. (The personal representative is an administrator with will annexed, not an administrator. The will was admitted on 12/24/18, not 8/10/18.)
